summary: Large decompilation/matching PR for Link's bomb item, adding an almost-complete C implementation of `src/melee/it/items/itlinkbomb.c` and the supporting item variable/attribute structs and helper prototypes needed to compile it. The PR title says it matched 98% of `itlinkbomb`; the diff shows 687 new lines in the C file, a populated `ItemStateTable`, Link bomb state callbacks from `it_8029D968` through the `it_8029FCxx` range, spawn/lifetime/explosion/collision handling, and several explicit matching workarounds using fake inlines, duplicated inline bodies, preprocessor switches, and comments about unresolved inline/lbcoll behavior. There were no review comments or PR body text in the provided slice.
what_changed: Replaced the previously empty/minimal `src/melee/it/items/itlinkbomb.c` with a 688-line implementation for Link bomb item behavior.
Added `ItemStateTable it_803F6888` with seven Link bomb item states and callbacks such as `it_8029E074`, `it_8029E3D8`, `it_8029E920`, `it_8029EF84`, `it_8029F3DC`, `it_8029F934`, and `it_8029FB4C`.
Added Link bomb item variable and special-attribute layouts in `itCharItems.h`: `itLinkBombVars` and `itLinkBombAttributes`.
Added prototypes needed by the new item implementation, including `it_8026DAA8`, `it_80272A60`, `it_80275414`, `it_802754A4`, and `it_80275D5C`.

Implemented Link bomb creation/spawn setup via `it_8029DD58`, including `SpawnItem` initialization, owner/fighter tracking, lifetime setup, animation offset setup, and `Item_8026AB54` ownership binding.

Implemented lifetime and animation ticking logic around `it_8029DB5C`, including transition to an animation state when the life timer reaches an attribute threshold and explosion/destruction when the timer reaches zero.

Implemented collision/bounce/explode behavior using item collision helpers, `mpColl_800436D8`, velocity comparisons against Link bomb attributes, and callbacks such as `it_8029EB3C`, `it_8029F098`, `it_8029F960`, and `it_8029FA30`.

Implemented explosion/destruction cleanup in `it_8029F69C`, including owner/held-item handling, `ftLk_AttackAir_SetupParts`, item cleanup helpers, hiding JObjs with `HSD_JObjSetFlagsAll`, and spawning a visual/effect call through `lb_800119DC`.
Added local fake inline wrappers for `HSD_JObjAddTranslationY` and `HSD_JObjAddRotationX` that call the deduplicated `ftCo_800C6AFC` path when `JOBJ_MTX_INDEP_SRT` is not set.

Added comments documenting known incomplete matching concerns: suspected header inline deduplication through `ftCo_800C6AFC`, dependence on future `lbcoll` matching, trouble making `it_8029E3D8` call a non-inlined `it_8029DB5C`, and a possible regswap related to fake inline use.
smart_moves: Encoded the Link bomb per-item state into `item->xDD4_itemVar.linkbomb` via a dedicated `itLinkBombVars` struct instead of leaving all accesses as raw offsets.
Separated special-attribute data into `itLinkBombAttributes`, making lifetime, velocity thresholds, bounce speeds, and timer thresholds searchable and reusable across item code.
Preserved animation bone transforms across state changes in `it_8029D9A4` by reading bone 3 translation Y and rotation X before `Item_80268E5C`, then restoring them afterward.
Used narrow fake inline wrappers for JObj translation/rotation updates rather than spreading the workaround throughout all call sites.
Kept alternate real-vs-matching implementations guarded with `TEST_REAL` and `#if` sections, making it explicit which code path exists for matching rather than behavior clarity.
Duplicated inline fragments such as timer-check and animation-add parts to influence compiler output and local register allocation in specific state callbacks.
Documented uncertain matching compromises directly next to the workaround code, especially around deduplicated inlines, `lbcoll`, and non-inlined `it_8029DB5C` behavior.
Added missing helper prototypes in subsystem headers instead of relying on implicit declarations, improving type safety for the newly decompiled item code.
decomp_lessons: For item actors with many similar state callbacks, matching may require intentionally duplicating shared helper logic or splitting an apparent helper into several inline-shaped fragments to reproduce compiler inlining decisions.
When a real library inline is not yet matched or has been deduplicated into another function, a local fake inline can be an acceptable temporary matching tactic if it is clearly named and commented.
State-change helpers can perturb animation/JObj fields; if the original preserves a transform across `Item_80268E5C`, explicitly save and restore the affected JObj fields.

Do not assume a clean non-inlined helper is usable just because several callbacks share logic; this PR shows `it_8029DB5C` logic being expanded manually because one or more callers did not match with a normal call.

Item special attributes should be modeled as a named struct as soon as enough offsets are known, even if many fields retain offset-like names, because the struct documents size/order and reduces repeated casts.

Comments that identify unresolved root causes, such as pending `lbcoll` work or suspected header inlines, are useful for future decompilers trying to replace fake inlines with canonical implementations.
Adding prototypes to coarse address-named headers like `it_266F.h` and `it_2725.h` is often necessary before item decompilation can proceed cleanly.
naming_conventions: Kept address-based function names for unmatched or not-yet-renamed functions, e.g. `it_8029D968`, `it_8029DD58`, `it_8029F69C`, `it_8026DAA8`, and `it_80272A60`.
Used item-specific struct names in camel/Pascal style: `itLinkBombVars` and `itLinkBombAttributes`.

Used explicit fake/matching helper names to mark noncanonical code, e.g. `fake_HSD_JObjAddTranslationY`, `fake_HSD_JObjAddRotationX`, `it_8029DB5C_Inline_Matching`, `it_8029DB5C_Inline_AnimAdd_Part`, and `it_8029DB5C_Inline_TimerCheck_Part`.
Retained offset-style field names for unknown Link bomb attribute and variable fields, such as `x4`, `x8`, `xC`, `x10`, `x24`, `x2C`, and bitfields under `x0.bits.b0/b1/b2`.
Named the state table by address as `it_803F6888` and the static vector as `it_803B8640`, following existing decomp symbol-address convention.
Used `sa` as a local variable for `itLinkBombAttributes*`, consistent with special-attributes shorthand seen in item code.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used fake JObj add-translation/add-rotation inlines that manually assert, mutate `jobj->translate.y` or `jobj->rotate.x`, and conditionally call `ftCo_800C6AFC` when the independent-SRT flag is absent.
Added `#define TEST_REAL 0` and `#if TEST_REAL` blocks to retain a cleaner helper-call version while compiling the manually expanded matching version.
Manually inlined `it_8029DB5C` logic in several state callbacks to match codegen where a direct function call apparently did not match.
Split shared timer and animation-add logic into small static inline fragments to control inlining and register behavior.
Inserted explicit temporary locals, padding arrays such as `int pad[5]` and `f32 pad[2]`, and staged `GET_ITEM`, `Article`, and `specialAttributes` loads to influence stack/register layout.
Used exact float constants such as `-2.8000002f`, `-0.15707964f`, `1.0471976f`, `0.02f`, and `0.0F` in places likely sensitive to generated code.
Used local inline helpers for sign and absolute-value logic, including `fsign_inline`, `float_sign_int_inline`, and `fabs_inline` usage.
Left comments identifying a possible regswap and unresolved non-inlined helper issue, indicating the 98% match was not fully clean.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR context.
No explicit reviewer feedback could be extracted from the slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that complete the remaining 2% of `itlinkbomb` or remove the fake JObj inline workarounds.
Search for later changes to `fake_HSD_JObjAddTranslationY`, `fake_HSD_JObjAddRotationX`, or `ftCo_800C6AFC` after `lbcoll` matching.
Search for PRs that rename `itLinkBombAttributes` fields from offset names to semantic names.
Search for changes to `src/sysdolphin/baselib/jobj.h` that add canonical `HSD_JObjAddTranslationY` or `HSD_JObjAddRotationX` inlines.
Compare other item PRs that manually inline shared lifetime/state-transition logic for matching.
